context.background: The Higgins Unity Framework (HUF) develops the Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer (EITT): Shannon entropy of compositional data is near-invariant under geometric-mean block decimation across temporal resolutions. Four empirical proofs validate this across electricity prices, generation mixes, climate scenarios, and chemical mixtures.
context.recent_synthesis: A shape/magnitude decomposition generalizes EITT beyond scalar entropy: any shape functional (entropy, correlation structure, principal balances, affinity clusters) is invariant under EITT decimation; any magnitude functional (Aitchison variance, total variation) scales as 1/M. The second-order Hessian bound has been proven under stated assumptions and checks against published proof data.
context.novelty_claim_under_test: A five-object cluster (EITT + Tutte + Hanani-Tutte + Penrose + KPZ) organized under this shape/magnitude decomposition is novel in the literature. Your branch tests whether the CoDa canon already contains any of these connections, especially in recent (2020-2025) work by the founding group.

your_branch_assignment.scope: Recent (2020-2025) publications by Pawlowsky-Glahn, Egozcue, Tolosana-Delgado, Ferrer-Rosell, Daunis-i-Estadella, Coenders, and related CoDa community authors. Check for mentions of: matching theory, parity arguments, aperiodic structure, self-similar scaling, universality classes, graph-theoretic existence conditions, or phase-field/phase-transition analogies applied to compositional data.
your_branch_assignment.why_chatgpt: Strong at sustained literature review with good recall across adjacent fields. Best-placed to build a matrix of recent CoDa publications and scan their abstracts and conclusions systematically for the specific concept markers we care about.

hypothesis_to_test.H1: The founding CoDa group (Pawlowsky-Glahn, Egozcue) has not published work connecting compositional data analysis to any of: Tutte matching, Hanani-Tutte parity, Penrose/aperiodic order, or KPZ universality, in the 2020-2025 window.
hypothesis_to_test.H2: The broader CoDa community (Ferrer-Rosell et al., Tolosana-Delgado et al.) has not connected compositional time series to these structural mathematical objects either.
hypothesis_to_test.H3: CoDa textbook coverage (Pawlowsky-Glahn, Egozcue & Tolosana-Delgado 2015 'Modeling and Analysis of Compositional Data') does not index or discuss any of the four mathematical objects.

tasks.action: List all papers from this window. For each, note title, co-authors, venue, and whether the abstract/keywords mention any of: matching, balance existence, parity, aperiodic, quasi-crystal, self-similar, universality, KPZ, graph, topology, Penrose. Flag anything relevant.
Same as T1. Their focus is often compositional time series, so most likely to have any time-series-related structural work.

tasks.target: Pawlowsky-Glahn, Egozcue, Tolosana-Delgado (2015) 'Modeling and Analysis of Compositional Data' (Wiley)
tasks.action: Check the book's index for entries on: matching, Tutte, Hanani-Tutte, graph theory, parity, Penrose, aperiodic, quasi-crystal, self-similar, KPZ, universality, Kardar-Parisi-Zhang, phase field, interface. Report which terms are indexed and cite the page ranges if possible.
tasks.access: If full text unavailable, try Google Books preview or publisher excerpts.

tasks.action: Scan abstract collections for talks mentioning any of the four mathematical objects or their adjacent concepts. CoDaWork abstracts are usually public. Report any abstracts that even tangentially touch these structures.
tasks.url_hint: https://www.coda-association.org hosts some proceedings; check past editions.

tasks.action: Report top 20 hits, classify by relevance.
Report top 20 hits, classify by relevance.
Report top 10 hits. Any positive hit is notable.
Based on T1-T9, assess H1, H2, H3 with confidence levels and short justifications. If the CoDa canon has any of these connections, the novelty claim must be downgraded.

required_fields.author_matrices: object with authors as keys, value is array of {title, year, venue, relevance_flags: [...], relevance_notes}
required_fields.search_results: array of {task_id, query, n_hits, top_hits, verdict}

verdict_definitions.positive: A CoDa-canon paper explicitly makes one of the four claimed connections. Novelty claim must be revised.
verdict_definitions.near_miss: A paper discusses adjacent concepts (e.g., self-similarity in compositional time series, or graph structure of balances) without completing the connection. Valuable for discussion section of any future paper.
verdict_definitions.negative: No relevant mentions found in the CoDa canon or recent work.

overlap_with_others.Claude: Claude is checking Tutte / Hanani-Tutte specifically in the combinatorial-graph-theory direction. If you find any SBP paper with graph-theoretic existence conditions, flag it — that overlaps with Claude's H3.
overlap_with_others.Grok: Grok is checking Penrose / aperiodic. If Pawlowsky-Glahn or Egozcue reference quasi-crystals or self-similar decomposition anywhere, flag it for Grok.
overlap_with_others.Gemini: Gemini is checking KPZ. If any CoDa paper cites Kardar-Parisi-Zhang, that is a strong finding worth escalating immediately.

coordination_notes.escalate_if: Any textbook index entry or author publication contains one of the four mathematical objects with substantive treatment (more than a passing citation).

estimated_effort: 2-3 hours: publication list builds can be sped up with Google Scholar author profiles, textbook review is bounded by book access, search tasks are quick.
